MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. VOLKOV LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 987-7000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS GOLDEN GATE HOTEL INC., JOHN KENASTON, RENATE KENASTON AND GABRIELE KENASTON's MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT is denied. Defendants do not assert proper grounds for a motion to strike. (See CCP 436 [The court may strike irrelevant, false, or improper matter, or any part of a pleading not drawn or filed in conformity with law.].). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. VOLKOV LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 987-7000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS GOLDEN GATE HOTEL INC., JOHN KENASTON, RENATE KENASTON AND GABRIELE KENASTON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - Demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. Plaintiff concedes that causes of action 2, 3, 5, 7, 9, 10, 15, and 19 are deficient, and as to those causes of action the Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to cause of action 8, which presumptively either Plaintiff intended to include in its list of admittedly deficient causes of action or otherwise does not oppose Defendants' grounds for demur. The Demurrer is also sustained with 10 days leave to amend in order for Plaintiff to clearly allege whether the contract at issue is written or oral. The Demurrer for uncertainty as to causes of action 1-12 and 14-20 is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Plaintiff to indicate which causes of action are brought against which Defendant(s), and to sufficiently allege a basis for liability against each Defendant for each cause of action. The Demurrer is also sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the uncertainty of which Wage Order allegedly applies to the employment relationship. The Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 1st and 4th causes of action. Plaintiff must plead in compliance with Oppenheimer v. Moebius (1957) 151 Cal.App.2d 818. As to the 6th cause of action, the Demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege an injury arising from the missing wage statements. The Demurrer as to the 11th cause of action is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege the Labor Code violation(s) that is the basis for this claim and the basis for Plaintiff's entitlement to restitution. As to the 12th cause of action, the Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff no longer works for Defendant and therefore would not suffer personal injury from continuing Labor Code violations. The Demurrer is overruled as to causes of action 14, 16, 17, 18 and 20 as the Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support each claim. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
